meanings.definition: Extremely ugly, unpleasant, or shocking to look at.
examples: The abandoned house looked hideous in the dark.
He wore a hideous sweater covered in bright colors.
The storm left behind a hideous mess in the streets.
She described the creature as the most hideous thing she had ever seen.

meanings.definition: Having a very unpleasant, harsh, or frightening sound.
examples: A hideous scream echoed through the empty building.
The machine made hideously loud noises all night.
Everyone covered their ears at the hideous sound of metal scraping.
The alarm produced a hideous ringing sound.

meanings.definition: Morally shocking, hateful, or deeply offensive.
examples: The community was horrified by the hideous act of violence.
People condemned the hideously cruel treatment of the animals.
The film showed the hideous reality of war.
He apologized for making such a hideous remark.

meanings.definition: Extremely evil, wicked, cruel, or morally terrible, especially used for crimes or actions.
examples: The police investigated the heinous crime for several months.
Everyone was shocked by the heinously violent attack.
The dictator was responsible for some of history’s most heinous acts.
She could not forgive such a heinous betrayal.

meanings.definition: Helped someone return to a healthy, normal, or improved condition after illness, injury, addiction, or difficulty.
examples: The athlete was rehabilitated after months of therapy.
Doctors are rehabilitating patients recovering from serious injuries.
The center rehabilitates people struggling with addiction.
He slowly rehabilitated himself after the accident.

meanings.definition: Restored someone’s reputation, public image, or social standing after damage or criticism.
examples: Historians later rehabilitated the reputation of the misunderstood leader.
The documentary helped rehabilitate her public image.
Writers are rehabilitating forgotten artists through modern research.
His name was finally rehabilitated after new evidence appeared.

meanings.definition: Repaired or restored a building, place, object, or system so that it could be used again.
examples: The old railway station was rehabilitated and reopened.
Workers are rehabilitating damaged roads after the flood.
The government rehabilitated several abandoned buildings.
The historic theater was beautifully rehabilitated.

meanings.definition: Trained or prepared someone, especially a criminal or offender, to successfully return to society and live responsibly.
examples: The prison program rehabilitated many young offenders.
Counselors work hard to rehabilitate troubled teenagers.
Several inmates were successfully rehabilitating through education programs.
The organization focuses on rehabilitating former addicts and prisoners.

meanings.definition: Recovered physically, mentally, or emotionally after a difficult condition or experience.
examples: She rehabilitated slowly after the surgery.
The injured bird was rehabilitating at the rescue center.
Many patients rehabilitate faster with family support.
He fully rehabilitated after years of treatment.
